reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes "a group of order 56 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order" as `∃ p, ∃ P : Sylow p G, p.Prime ∧ p ∣ card G ∧ P.Normal`. The hypothesis `hG : card G = 56` fixes the group order. The conjuncts `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G` capture "for some prime p dividing its order", while `∃ (P : Sylow p G), P.Normal` captures "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup". Including `p ∣ card G` is necessary because Mathlib's `Sylow p G` type is defined for any natural number p (as a maximal p-subgroup), and for primes p not dividing |G| the trivial subgroup is trivially a normal Sylow p-subgroup — so this constraint matches the informal quantifier. The quantifier structure and all ambient objects match the informal claim.
